perturbation.original_text: 2.1 The receiving party agrees: (a) to keep the disclosing party’s confidential information, confidential; (b) not to use the disclosing party’s confidential information except in connection with the purpose; and (c) not to disclose the disclosing party’s confidential information to anyone else.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 The receiving party agrees: (a) to keep the disclosing party’s confidential information, confidential; (b) not to use the disclosing party’s confidential information except in connection with the purpose, unless explicit approval has been granted by the receiving party's legal counsel; and (c) not to disclose the disclosing party’s confidential information to anyone else.
perturbation.explanation: The added clause to section 2.1(b) creates an in-text contradiction. It allows the receiving party to use the disclosing party's confidential information for purposes beyond the defined "purpose" with the approval of their legal counsel. This contradicts the original restriction that strictly limited use to the defined "purpose," thus making the scope of permitted use ambiguous and potentially leading to disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 The receiving party agrees: (a) to keep the disclosing party’s confidential information, confidential; (b) not to use the disclosing party’s confidential information except in connection with the purpose, unless explicit approval has been granted by the receiving party's legal counsel; and (c) not to disclose the disclosing party’s confidential information to anyone else.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause that allows the receiving party's legal counsel to grant approval for using the disclosing party's confidential information for purposes beyond the defined "purpose." This directly contradicts the original restriction that strictly limited use to the defined "purpose." The contradicted text, which is actually the same as the changed text, highlights this contradiction because it now contains conflicting conditions for the use of confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: 4 General This agreement forms the entire agreement between the parties relating to confidential information disclosed in connection with the purpose. It replaces any earlier agreements, representations or discussions. Neither party excludes liability for any fraud.
perturbation.changed_text: 4 General This agreement forms the entire agreement between the parties relating to confidential information disclosed in connection with the purpose, unless otherwise stipulated in a separate side letter executed by both parties. It replaces any earlier agreements, representations or discussions, except for those pertaining to intellectual property ownership explicitly retained by either party prior to the effective date of this agreement. Neither party excludes liability for any fraud.
perturbation.explanation: The modification to Section 4 introduces an in-text contradiction. The original clause states that the agreement is the 'entire agreement,' but the changed text allows for exceptions via 'separate side letter' and 'intellectual property ownership'. This creates uncertainty about the scope of the agreement and which terms prevail, thus contradicting the original intention of the clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4 General This agreement forms the entire agreement between the parties relating to confidential information disclosed in connection with the purpose, unless otherwise stipulated in a separate side letter executed by both parties. It replaces any earlier agreements, representations or discussions, except for those pertaining to intellectual property ownership explicitly retained by either party prior to the effective date of this agreement. Neither party excludes liability for any fraud.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 4 to allow exceptions to the 'entire agreement' clause through 'separate side letter' and 'intellectual property ownership'. This directly contradicts the original intent of the 'entire agreement' clause as it creates uncertainty about the scope of the agreement and which terms prevail, resulting in an in-text contradiction as defined by the provided 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, highlighting that the change introduces the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2.4 Nothing i n this agreement will prevent the receiving party from disclosing confidential information: (a) if the disclosing party has given its prior written consent; or (b) to its lawyers; or (c) if the disclosure is required by law, regulation or professional requirements; or (d) in the case of PwC, to other PwC firms or relevant subcontractors.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.4 Nothing i n this agreement will prevent the receiving party from disclosing confidential information: (a) if the disclosing party has given its prior written consent; or (b) to its lawyers, provided they sign a separate confidentiality agreement with the disclosing party; or (c) if the disclosure is required by law, regulation or professional requirements; or (d) in the case of PwC, only to other PwC firms after notifying the disclosing party in writing.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'provided they sign a separate confidentiality agreement with the disclosing party' for disclosures to lawyers (2.4(b)) and 'only to other PwC firms after notifying the disclosing party in writing' (2.4(d)) introduces an in-text contradiction. Originally, these disclosures were permitted without additional conditions. Now, they are subject to new requirements, creating ambiguity about which terms apply and potentially hindering necessary disclosures. The phrase contradicts the original intent of unrestricted sharing as stated in the original section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.4 Nothing i n this agreement will prevent the receiving party from disclosing confidential information: (a) if the disclosing party has given its prior written consent; or (b) to its lawyers, provided they sign a separate confidentiality agreement with the disclosing party; or (c) if the disclosure is required by law, regulation or professional requirements; or (d) in the case of PwC, only to other PwC firms after notifying the disclosing party in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds conditions to the disclosure of confidential information to lawyers and other PwC firms that were not present in the original text. Specifically, it requires lawyers to sign a separate confidentiality agreement and PwC firms to notify the disclosing party in writing. This contradicts the original section 2.4, which allows for disclosure to these parties without these conditions. The addition of these requirements creates a direct conflict and inconsistency within the document, making it unclear which terms apply and potentially hindering necessary disclosures, as the contradicted text is located in the same place.

perturbation.original_text: 1 Definitions In this agreement the fo llowing words have the meanings given to them below: “confidential information” means any information disclosed by one party to the other party in connection with the purpose, which is of a confidential nature irrespective of whether it is marked as such .
perturbation.changed_text: 1 Definitions In this agreement the fo llowing words have the meanings given to them below: “confidential information” means any information disclosed by one party to the other party in connection with the purpose, which is of a confidential nature only if it is marked as such .
perturbation.explanation: The modification of the definition of "confidential information" in Section 1 introduces a contradiction. Originally, confidential information was defined as such 'irrespective of whether it is marked as such.' The changed text now states it is only confidential 'if it is marked as such.' This creates a direct contradiction, leading to uncertainty about what information is protected under the agreement, and rendering other sections which rely on the original definition uncertain and potentially unenforceable. The scope is entirely changed by this perturbation and leads to in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1 Definitions In this agreement the fo llowing words have the meanings given to them below: “confidential information” means any information disclosed by one party to the other party in connection with the purpose, which is of a confidential nature only if it is marked as such .
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'confidential information' from including information of a confidential nature 'irrespective of whether it is marked as such' to only including information of a confidential nature 'only if it is marked as such.' This creates a direct contradiction, rendering the scope and enforceability of the definition unclear. Therefore, this is a clear in-text contradiction.
